commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=99b33ae94005d3e90e1ef1b3e7a1ffd4dd192057)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a core `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list` CLI, a local SQLite snapshot repository/provider, named global and per-agent database targets, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +730, Tests +716, Docs +103. Total +1549 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main correctness blocker: adapt the existing backup symlink regression to `snapshot create --target global`, then restore/read the copied artifact. I did not execute it because this review is read-only, but current backup source and tests show the invariant the PR misses.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR is draft/protected and needs contributor fixes plus maintainer acceptance of the new public CLI/artifact contract before merge.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body: ## SummaryThis replaces the earlier PR1-4 stack with one coherent core snapshot MVP.Adds:- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files- safe-sync workflow proof: create a snapshot, copy only the completed artifact directory, restore from that copy, and verify the restored SQLite database- CLI docs that say which files are syncable and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored- CLI docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup`: snapshot is a per-database SQLite sync artifact; backup is a broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive## WhyThe operational goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.This PR establishes that boundary:- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`- hosts/containers/object-store sync jobs should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens itThis is intentionally different from `openclaw backup`: backup remains the broad local recovery archive for OpenClaw state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces. Snapshot is the narrow SQLite-safe artifact that backup systems or host sync loops can copy safely.## StackThis PR now combines the previous core implementation slices:- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets- this PR: safe-sync artifact and restore proof/docsFollow-up validation PR: #94967RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20Related: #94675, #94646## ScopeThis does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.## Real behavior proofBehavior or issue addressed:OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database. The packaged CLI path now proves `snapshot create`, `snapshot verify`, and `snapshot restore` from `openclaw.mjs`, not only direct source-harness calls.Real environment tested:Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node v22.22.3, `pnpm build` packaged output, `node openclaw.mjs` launcher commands, temporary local SQLite database created with Node `node:sqlite`, local snapshot repository under `.artifacts/snapshot-proof/pr94805`, copied/restored local SQLite target, plus source Vitest harnesses with WAL-mode SQLite databases and named OpenClaw database targets.Exact steps or command run after this patch:1. `pnpm build`2. Create a temporary SQLite DB with `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, `PRAGMA user_version = 314`, and rows `before-wal` / `committed-in-wal`.3. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create --db <proof-root>/source.sqlite --repository <proof-root>/snapshots --id proof-db --kind operator-proof --json`4. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --json`5. Create stale restore-side `<target>.sqlite-wal` and `<target>.sqlite-shm` files, then run `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --target <proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite --json`.6. Read the restored DB with Node `node:sqlite` and query `SELECT value FROM entries ORDER BY id` plus `PRAGMA user_version`.7. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`8. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/docs.json docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/backup.md docs/cli/index.md`9. `git diff --check`10. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`11. `pnpm docs:check-links`12. `pnpm docs:list`13. Codex review: `codex review --base origin/giodl/snapshot-named-targets-pr3`, then rerun after the docs navigation fix. Final PR A review: `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues.Evidence after fix:Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create ... --json` returned `ok: true`, a snapshot path under `<proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id>`, `database.id: "proof-db"`, `database.kind: "operator-proof"`, `database.userVersion: 314`, artifact path `database.sqlite`, artifact size `8192`, and a `sha256:<64 hex chars>` artifact hash.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify ... --json` returned `ok: true` and `integrityCheck: ["ok"]` for the same manifest and snapshot path.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore ... --json` returned `ok: true`, `integrityCheck: ["ok"]`, the same snapshot path, and target path `<proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite`.Readback from the restored SQLite DB returned:```json{  "rows": [    { "value": "before-wal" },    { "value": "committed-in-wal" }  ],  "userVersion": { "user_version": 314 },  "walExistsAfterRestore": false,  "shmExistsAfterRestore": false}```The created snapshot directory contained exactly:```json[  { "Name": "database.sqlite", "Length": 8192 },  { "Name": "manifest.json", "Length": 455 }]```Supporting validation passed: Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests). `pnpm build`, `oxfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, `docs:check-mdx`, `docs:check-links`, and `docs:list` completed cleanly. Codex review initially found one P3 docs navigation gap; adding `cli/snapshot` to `docs/docs.json` cleared the rerun with no actionable findings. A follow-up docs-only check also passed for the snapshot-versus-backup clarification. Final `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues.Observed result after fix:The packaged CLI created a verified snapshot artifact from a SQLite database, verified the artifact hash and SQLite integrity, restored the artifact to a fresh SQLite file, removed stale restore-side WAL/SHM files, and preserved both expected rows plus `PRAGMA user_version = 314`. The artifact directory exposed the intended syncable contract: `manifest.json` plus `database.sqlite`, with no live SQLite sidecar files as part of the artifact.The source tests additionally create WAL-mode SQLite databases, produce snapshot artifact directories, verify manifests and hashes, list snapshots, restore into fresh targets, remove stale restore-side sidecars, run SQLite integrity checks, and read expected restored rows. The named-target tests cover both `--target global` and `--agent <id>` while preserving the explicit `--db` path.What was not tested:No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: giodl73-repo
